steps.title: Sketch the seat and back rail (pencil)
steps.description: Lightly sketch two long horizontal lines — the seat at the bottom, the back rail a bit higher and slightly behind it. Keep them roughly parallel. These two lines are the whole bench; everything else hangs from them.

steps.title: Add the legs (pencil)
steps.description: Sketch four vertical lines dropping from the underside of the seat — two at each end, slightly inside the seat edges. Make them equal length so the bench feels level.

steps.title: Add the armrests (pencil)
steps.description: At each end of the seat, sketch a short horizontal stroke for the armrest and a small vertical connector joining it to the seat. Armrests sit just above seat height.

steps.title: Switch to ink and trace the seat and back rail
steps.description: Switch to ink. Draw each rail as one confident horizontal stroke from end to end. The back rail is the longer stroke — let it extend a hair beyond the armrest on each side to give the bench a solid feel.

steps.title: Ink the legs and armrests (ink)
steps.description: Ink each leg with a single downward stroke, stopping cleanly at the ground. Notice where the legs meet the seat above — the junction should be a clean T, not a blur. Finish with the armrests and their connectors.
